content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "document-management",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 32,
    "domain_depth": 35,
    "data_richness": 28,
    "interaction_quality": 30,
    "ai_integration": 22,
    "mobile_first": 18,
    "performance": 35,
    "typescript_quality": 45,
    "error_handling": 38,
    "realtime": 25,
    "accessibility": 15,
    "security": 40,
    "differentiation": 20,
    "enterprise_readiness": 30
  },
  "overall_score": 30,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "document-management-C6-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Drawing viewer lacks fabric.js annotation layer for field markup",
      "file": "src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx",
      "description": "The DrawingViewer component renders PDFs but has no actual annotation/markup capability using fabric.js or any canvas overlay. Superintendents need to mark up drawings on iPads in the field. This is P0-3 from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP.md and the #1 question in every demo.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx, in the DrawingViewer component: BEFORE: The component renders a PDF using PdfViewer but has no interactive annotation layer. There is no fabric.js canvas overlay, no markup state management, and no persistence of annotations. The MarkupToolbar exists as a separate component but is not wired to an actual drawing canvas. AFTER: Install fabric (npm install fabric @types/fabric). Add a fabric.Canvas overlay positioned absolutely on top of the PDF render area. Initialize the canvas on PDF page load, sized to match the PDF viewport. Add state for activeAnnotationTool (pen, highlighter, text, rectangle, circle, arrow, cloud) and activeColor. When a tool is selected, configure fabric drawing mode accordingly: for pen use freeDrawingBrush with width 3, for highlighter use freeDrawingBrush with width 20 and opacity 0.3, for shapes add fabric objects on mouse events. Add an undo stack (array of JSON snapshots, max 20) that pushes on every object:added and path:created event. Expose onAnnotationsChange callback that serializes canvas to JSON. Add a floating save button that calls onSave with the serialized annotation_data. Add props: drawingId (string), pageNumber (number), isEditable (boolean), initialAnnotations (JSON | null), onSave ((data: object) => void). When isEditable is false, load annotations as non-selectable objects. Render the MarkupToolbar below the viewer only when isEditable is true, passing activeAnnotationTool and onToolChange. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C6-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Drawings page shows no revision history timeline or linked RFI/submittal counts",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "The Drawings page lists drawings but does not display revision count, linked RFI count, linked submittal count, or a revision history timeline per the PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA. These are required fields per criteria #2, #4, and #6 for the Drawings page.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the default exported component: BEFORE: The drawing list/grid shows basic drawing info (sheet number, title, discipline) but is missing columns for revision number, linked RFIs count, linked submittals count, and last viewed timestamp. There are no metric cards at the top of the page showing total drawings, drawings by discipline, or pending revisions. AFTER: Add a metric cards row at the top with 4 MetricBox components: Total Drawings (count of all drawings), Current Revisions (count with currentRevision not null), Pending Markups (sum of markup_count > 0), and Disciplines (count of unique discipline values). In the drawing list table or grid cards, add columns/fields for: Revision (show currentRevision?.revision_number or 'Rev 0'), Linked RFIs (query rfis table where drawing references match, show count as blue badge), Date Issued (currentRevision?.issued_date formatted), and Status badge (current/superseded/draft from drawing status field). Add discipline color coding using getDisciplineColor from the documents endpoint — render a colored dot or pill next to the discipline name. For each drawing card in grid view, show the revision count as 'Rev X' in the top right corner. Ensure the empty state follows acceptance criteria: 'No drawings uploaded yet. Upload your plans to enable digital markup, RFI linking, and AI coordination analysis.' with an Upload Drawings CTA button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C6-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Files page has no drag-and-drop upload zone or folder navigation",
      "file": "src/pages/Files.tsx",
      "description": "The Files page lacks a drag-and-drop upload area (UploadZone component exists but may not be integrated), folder tree navigation for the hierarchical folder structure returned by getFiles, and inline file preview. These are fundamental document management interactions.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Files.tsx, in the default exported component: BEFORE: The Files page renders a flat list of files without folder hierarchy navigation. There is no drag-and-drop upload zone visible on the page. File rows are not clickable to open a preview. There are no breadcrumbs showing the current folder path. AFTER: Import the UploadZone component from src/components/files/UploadZone.tsx and render it at the top of the page as a collapsible drop zone (shown by default when folder is empty, toggleable via an 'Upload' button otherwise). Add folder navigation state: currentFolderId (string | null, null = root). Filter the MappedFile[] from getFiles to show only items where parent_folder_id matches currentFolderId. Render folders first (items where isFolder is true) as clickable rows with a Folder icon — clicking sets currentFolderId to that folder's id. Add breadcrumb navigation above the file list showing the path from root to current folder, each segment clickable to navigate up. For file rows, make the entire row clickable — clicking a non-folder file opens the FilePreview component (from src/components/files/FilePreview.tsx) in a slide-over Drawer. Add file type icons based on content_type: PDF gets FileText icon in red, images get Image icon in blue, spreadsheets get Table icon in green, other gets File icon in gray. Add a search input at the top that filters files by name across all folders. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C6-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Drawing viewer and file list lack ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, and focus management",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "The Drawings page has no ARIA role annotations on the drawing grid/list, no keyboard navigation between drawing cards, no aria-live region for filter result count changes, and no skip navigation for the discipline filter pills. This fails the global accessibility standards.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the default exported component: BEFORE: The drawing list has no role='grid' or role='list' on the container. Drawing cards have no role='article' or aria-label describing the drawing. Discipline filter pills have no role='group' with aria-label. There is no aria-live region announcing filter result counts. Tab order does not follow visual order through metric cards then filters then drawings. AFTER: Add role='list' and aria-label='Project drawings' to the drawings container div. Add role='listitem' to each drawing card/row. Add aria-label to each card: `${drawing.title}, ${drawing.discipline}, Revision ${rev}`. Wrap the discipline filter pills in a div with role='group' and aria-label='Filter by discipline'. Add an aria-live='polite' sr-only span that updates with 'Showing X of Y drawings' whenever the filtered list changes. Add tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handler to each drawing card: Enter opens the drawing viewer, Space toggles selection. Add a visually hidden h1 at the top: 'Drawings' for screen reader page identification. Ensure all icon-only buttons (upload, grid/list toggle, search clear) have aria-label attributes. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C6-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Drawing viewer not optimized for mobile pinch-to-zoom and touch markup",
      "file": "src/components/drawings/PdfViewer.tsx",
      "description": "The PdfViewer component does not handle touch events for pinch-to-zoom, has no responsive canvas sizing for mobile viewports, and the toolbar is not adapted for touch targets. Per acceptance criteria, drawings must support pinch-to-zoom on mobile with markup tools in a collapsible bottom toolbar.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/drawings/PdfViewer.tsx, in the PdfViewer component: BEFORE: The component renders a canvas for the PDF but does not handle touch gestures for pinch-to-zoom. The toolbar renders at a fixed position not optimized for mobile. There is no viewport meta handling and canvas dimensions are not responsive to container width. Touch targets for page navigation buttons are not 44x44px minimum. AFTER: Add a container div with style overflow='hidden' and position='relative' that fills the available width. Calculate canvas scale based on container width: on mount and resize, measure container offsetWidth and set scale = containerWidth / pdfPageWidth. Add touch event handling for pinch-to-zoom: track two-finger touches, calculate distance delta, and adjust a zoomLevel state (min 0.5, max 5.0) which multiplies the base scale. Apply CSS transform: `scale(${zoomLevel})` with transformOrigin='0 0' on the canvas wrapper. Add touch-action='none' to prevent browser default pinch behavior. Make page navigation buttons at least 44px height and width with padding. On viewports below 768px (use window.matchMedia or the useMediaQuery hook), move the toolbar to a fixed bottom bar with 56px height, horizontally scrollable tool icons at 44x44px each. Add a double-tap handler that resets zoomLevel to 1.0 (zoom to fit). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "AI Drawing Coordination Scanner",
      "description": "When a new drawing revision is uploaded, an edge function uses Claude Vision to compare it against the previous revision and cross-reference open RFIs in that area. It surfaces a panel: 'This revision shifted the beam at grid C-4 by 6 inches. 2 open RFIs reference this area. 1 approved submittal for steel fabrication may be affected.' This is the #1 feature no competitor ships and directly addresses the 'AI Conflict Detection' vision item.",
      "effort": "large"
    },
    {
      "title": "Drawing Hotspot Heatmap",
      "description": "Aggregate all RFI locations, punch items, and markups pinned to each drawing and render a heatmap overlay showing concentration of issues. Red zones indicate areas with the most coordination problems. Helps PMs identify problematic building areas at a glance.",
      "effort": "medium"
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's document viewer is functional but lacks AI analysis. Fieldwire has excellent task-to-plan linking with pin drops but no AI conflict detection. PlanGrid (now part of ACC) has the best markup tools in the industry with Apple Pencil support and multi-page comparison. SiteSync must match PlanGrid's markup quality (fabric.js can do this) while adding AI coordination analysis that nobody ships today. The revision comparison slider and AI conflict detection would be genuine differentiators.",
  "summary": "The document management module is at 30/100, primarily because the drawing viewer lacks the annotation layer that is the #1 superintendent requirement (P0-3 from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP), the Files page has no folder navigation or drag-and-drop upload integration, and both pages are missing the metric cards, linked entity counts, and revision history views required by acceptance criteria. The TypeScript layer is decent thanks to the well-typed documents.ts endpoint that handles revision grouping and discipline mapping, but the frontend components do not consume this data richness. The most impactful fix is implementing the fabric.js annotation layer in DrawingViewer.tsx, followed by wiring up the revision and linked-item data into the Drawings page UI, and adding proper folder navigation to the Files page. Mobile support and accessibility are both critically deficient."
}
```
